description: §38.6.1 clean neg — the same server-escalated channel function broadcasts a value derived from its ARGUMENT (`delta`) instead of reading the channel cell. This is the SPEC-recommended shape (operate on the payload/args), so E-CHANNEL-SERVER-CELL-READ stays silent. The `@count` cell is read only on the CLIENT (`${@count}`), which is permitted. Delta from `channel/server-cell-read`: arg-read vs channel-cell-read in the server function.
rationale: §38.6.1: a server-context channel function that operates on its arguments / the message payload (not a channel cell) is well-formed. A client-side read of a channel cell is unaffected. E-CHANNEL-SERVER-CELL-READ does not fire.
